sections.title: The Parable of the Lost Sheep
The Parable of the Unforgiving Servant

verses.1: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, “Who then is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?”
verses.2: Jesus invited a little child to stand among them.

segments.text: “Truly I tell you, unless you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

4.text: Therefore, whoever humbles himself like this little child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
4.speaker: Jesus

5.text: And whoever welcomes a little child like this in My name welcomes Me.
5.speaker: Jesus

6.text: But if anyone causes one of these little ones who believe in Me to stumble, it would be better for him to have a large millstone hung around his neck and to be drowned in the depths of the sea.
6.speaker: Jesus

7.text: Woe to the world for the causes of sin. These stumbling blocks must come, but woe to the man through whom they come!
7.speaker: Jesus

8.text: If your hand or your foot causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. It is better for you to enter life crippled or lame than to have two hands and two feet and be thrown into the eternal fire.
8.speaker: Jesus

9.text: And if your eye causes you to sin, gouge it out and throw it away. It is better for you to enter life with one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fire of hell.
9.speaker: Jesus

10.text: See that you do not look down on any of these little ones. For I tell you that their angels in heaven always see the face of My Father in heaven.
10.speaker: Jesus

11.text: For the Son of Man came to save that which was lost.
11.speaker: Jesus

12.text: What do you think? If a man has a hundred sheep and one of them goes astray, will he not leave the ninety-nine on the hills and go out to search for the one that is lost?
12.speaker: Jesus

13.text: And if he finds it, truly I tell you, he rejoices more over that one sheep than over the ninety-nine that did not go astray.
13.speaker: Jesus

14.text: In the same way, your Father in heaven is not willing that any of these little ones should perish.
14.speaker: Jesus

15.text: If your brother sins against you, go and confront him privately. If he listens to you, you have won your brother over.
15.speaker: Jesus

16.text: But if he will not listen, take one or two others along, so that ‘every matter may be established by the testimony of two or three witnesses.’
16.speaker: Jesus

17.text: If he refuses to listen to them, tell it to the church. And if he refuses to listen even to the church, regard him as you would a pagan or a tax collector.
17.speaker: Jesus

18.text: Truly I tell you, whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.
18.speaker: Jesus

19.text: Again, I tell you truly that if two of you on the earth agree about anything you ask for, it will be done for you by My Father in heaven.
19.speaker: Jesus

20.text: For where two or three gather together in My name, there am I with them.”
20.speaker: Jesus

verses.21: Then Peter came to Jesus and asked, “Lord, how many times shall I forgive my brother who sins against me? Up to seven times?”

segments.text: “I tell you, not just seven times, but seventy-seven times!
segments.speaker: Jesus

23.text: Because of this, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
23.speaker: Jesus

24.text: As he began the settlements, a debtor owing ten thousand talents was brought to him.
24.speaker: Jesus

25.text: Since the man was unable to pay, the master ordered that he be sold to pay his debt, along with his wife and children and everything he owned.
25.speaker: Jesus

26.text: Then the servant fell on his knees before him. ‘Have patience with me,’ he begged, ‘and I will pay back everything.’
26.speaker: Jesus

27.text: His master had compassion on him, forgave his debt, and released him.
27.speaker: Jesus

28.text: But when that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him a hundred denarii. He grabbed him and began to choke him, saying, ‘Pay back what you owe me!’
28.speaker: Jesus

29.text: So his fellow servant fell down and begged him, ‘Have patience with me, and I will pay you back.’
29.speaker: Jesus

30.text: But he refused. Instead, he went and had the man thrown into prison until he could pay his debt.
30.speaker: Jesus

31.text: When his fellow servants saw what had happened, they were greatly distressed, and they went and recounted all of this to their master.
31.speaker: Jesus

32.text: Then the master summoned him and said, ‘You wicked servant! I forgave all your debt because you begged me.
32.speaker: Jesus

33.text: Shouldn’t you have had mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had on you?’
33.speaker: Jesus

34.text: In anger his master turned him over to the jailers to be tortured, until he should repay all that he owed.
34.speaker: Jesus

35.text: That is how My heavenly Father will treat each of you unless you forgive your brother from your heart.”
35.speaker: Jesus

metadata.source: Derived from the Berean Standard Bible (BSB). Missing verses restored from the Textus Receptus tradition for completeness.
